# Netflix Surpasses 301 Million Paid Memberships

 Editor

Netflix, the world’s leading streaming service, reported fourth quarter earnings for 2024 and says it has over 301 million paid subscribers worldwide, a 15.9% increase from last year.

Revenues for the fourth quarter of 2024 were $10.25 billion, a 16% increase. Net income was $1.87 billion.", "score": 0.88799405, "raw_content": null}, {"url": "https://projectcasting.com/blog/news/netflix-ends-2024-with-301-million-subscribers-breaking-records", "title": "Netflix Ends 2024 With 301 Million Subscribers, Breaking Records - Project Casting", "content": "### Key Takeaways:

 Netflix gained a record-breaking 18.91 million subscribers in Q4 2024, reaching 301.63 million global subscribers.
 The platform reported a 15.9% year-over-year growth in subscriptions.
 Major hits like Squid Game Season 2 and Carry-On fueled subscriber growth and engagement.
 Netflix plans to release the final seasons of Stranger Things and Squid Game in 2025, along with new gaming initiatives.", "score": 0.8703443, "raw_content": null}, {"url": "https://mediabrief.com/netflix-adds-19mn-subscribers-in-q4-reaches-301-6mn-total", "title": "Netflix adds 19mn subscribers in Q4, reaches 301.6mn total", "content": "Operating income surpassed $10 billion for the first time in Netflix’s history. The fourth quarter marked a particularly strong performance, with revenue rising 16% year over year, driven by 19 (18.91) million paid net additions, while operating income surged 52% compared to the same period in 2023. Netflix ended the year with 300 (301.63) million memberships.", "score": 0.8127637, "raw_content": null}, {"url": "https://www.nasdaq.com/articles/netflix-nflx-94-last-earnings-report-can-it-continue", "title": "Netflix (NFLX) Up 9.4% Since Last Earnings Report: Can It Continue? | Nasdaq", "content": "The company added 18.91 million subscribers during its fourth quarter (the biggest quarter of net adds in the company’s history) compared with 13.12 million net new subscribers in the year-ago period.  
  
The average revenue per membership (ARM) was up 1% year over year and 3% on foreign-exchange neutral basis in the fourth quarter.  
  
At the end of the fourth quarter, Netflix had 301.63 million paid subscribers across more than 190 countries globally, up 15.9% year over year.", "score": 0.7680558, "raw_content": null}, {"url": "https://thedesk.net/2024/10/netflix-q3-2024-earnings-report", "title": "Netflix adds 5 million global subscribers during Q3 2024", "content": "Netflix said its revenue in the U.S. and Canada grew 16 percent on a year-over basis, driven by a 10 percent increase in average paid memberships, among other factors. In Europe, the Middle East and Africa, revenue there also grew 16 percent on a year-over basis, while in Latin America, revenue increased 9 percent.", "score": 0.657561, "raw_content": null}, {"url": "https://www.pymnts.com/earnings/2021/netflix-hits-208-million-paid-memberships-as-growth-slows", "title": "PYMNTS | Netflix Hits 208 Million Paid Memberships", "content": "And, in terms of competition, Netflix reported that additional new streaming platforms are rolling out. The company said in the letter that the trend supports its “vision that linear TV will slowly give way to streaming entertainment.”

“Despite our 10+ year head start, we are continually seeking operational and creative excellence; we’re working as hard as ever to continually improve our service so that we are the best entertainment option available,” the company wrote in the letter.", "score": 0.36929289, "raw_content": null}, {"url": "https://www.thenomadtoday.com/articulo/economy-business/netflix-quarterly-earnings-shows-25-billion-dollars-in-annual-revenue/20210120094400010034.html", "title": "Netflix quarterly earnings shows 25 billion dollars in annual revenue", "content": ""For the full year, we added a record 37m paid memberships, achieved $25 billion in annual revenue (+24% year over year) and grew operating profit 76% to $4.6 billion," the company wrote in a letter to shareholders.

Netflix announced that it expects to break even on cash flow this year, meaning that it will make enough money to run and reinvest in the business, and that it expects to be free cash flow positive after 2021.

The company plans to release one new film every week throughout 2021.", "score": 0.34125364, "raw_content": null}]

steps.args: {'prompt': 'From Netflix 2024 10-K {{nflx_2024_10k_htm}}, extract annual revenue for 2024, 2023, 2022 and any paid memberships or average paid memberships metrics, average revenue per membership/user for 2024/2023/2022 if available. Provide exact numbers and text context.'}
steps.result:
Below are the requested figures extracted from Netflix’s 2024 Form 10-K. Dollar amounts are **in thousands** unless otherwise noted; membership figures are **in thousands** unless otherwise noted.

## Text context from the 10-K

Netflix states:

> “The following represents our consolidated performance highlights:”

and presents annual revenue, paid membership, average paying membership, and average monthly revenue per paying membership metrics for 2024, 2023, and 2022.

Netflix also defines paid membership as:

> “A paid membership (also referred to as a paid subscription) is defined as a membership that has the right to receive Netflix service following sign-up and a method of payment being provided, and that is not part of a free trial or certain other promotions that may be offered by the Company to new or rejoining members.”

For revenue recognition, Netflix states:

> “The Company’s primary source of revenues is from monthly membership fees. Members are billed in advance of the start of their monthly membership and revenues are recognized ratably over each monthly membership period.”

---

## Annual revenue — consolidated

| Year | Streaming revenues | DVD revenues | Total revenues |
|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| 2024 | $39,000,966 | $— | $39,000,966 |
| 2023 | $33,640,458 | $82,839 | $33,723,297 |
| 2022 | $31,469,852 | $145,698 | $31,615,550 |

Text context:

> “We discontinued our DVD-by-mail service in the year ended December 31, 2023. The discontinuance of our DVD business had an immaterial impact on our operations and financial results.”

---

## Global paid membership metrics

| Year | Paid net membership additions | Paid memberships at end of period | Average paying memberships | Average monthly revenue per paying membership |
|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| 2024 | 41,350 | 301,626 | 277,730 | $11.70 |
| 2023 | 29,529 | 260,276 | 240,889 | $11.64 |
| 2022 | 8,903 | 230,747 | 222,924 | $11.76 |

In millions, Netflix ended 2024 with approximately **301.6 million paid memberships**, versus **260.3 million** in 2023 and **230.7 million** in 2022.

Text context:

> “Streaming revenues for the year ended December 31, 2024 increased 16% as compared to the year ended December 31, 2023, primarily due to the growth in average paying memberships and price increases, partially offset by unfavorable changes in foreign exchange rates.”

---

## Regional streaming revenue, paid memberships, average paying memberships, and ARM

### United States and Canada — UCAN

| Year | Streaming revenues | Paid net membership additions / losses | Paid memberships at end of period | Average paying memberships | Average monthly revenue per paying membership |
|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| 2024 | $17,359,369 | 9,497 | 89,625 | 84,112 | $17.20 |
| 2023 | $14,873,783 | 5,832 | 80,128 | 76,126 | $16.28 |
| 2022 | $14,084,643 | (919) | 74,296 | 74,001 | $15.86 |

---

### Europe, Middle East, and Africa — EMEA

| Year | Streaming revenues | Paid net membership additions | Paid memberships at end of period | Average paying memberships | Average monthly revenue per paying membership |
|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| 2024 | $12,387,035 | 12,320 | 101,133 | 94,200 | $10.96 |
| 2023 | $10,556,487 | 12,084 | 88,813 | 80,928 | $10.87 |
| 2022 | $9,745,015 | 2,693 | 76,729 | 73,904 | $10.99 |

---

### Latin America — LATAM

| Year | Streaming revenues | Paid net membership additions | Paid memberships at end of period | Average paying memberships | Average monthly revenue per paying membership |
|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| 2024 | $4,839,816 | 7,330 | 53,327 | 48,954 | $8.24 |
| 2023 | $4,446,461 | 4,298 | 45,997 | 42,802 | $8.66 |
| 2022 | $4,069,973 | 1,738 | 41,699 | 40,000 | $8.48 |

---

### Asia-Pacific — APAC

| Year | Streaming revenues | Paid net membership additions | Paid memberships at end of period | Average paying memberships | Average monthly revenue per paying membership |
|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| 2024 | $4,414,746 | 12,203 | 57,541 | 50,466 | $7.29 |
| 2023 | $3,763,727 | 7,315 | 45,338 | 41,033 | $7.64 |
| 2022 | $3,570,221 | 5,391 | 38,023 | 35,019 | $8.50 |

---

## Notes on ARPU / ARM terminology

Netflix reports this metric as:

> “Average monthly revenue per paying membership”

This is effectively Netflix’s disclosed ARPU-like metric, often referred to by Netflix as **ARM**. The global figures disclosed were:

- **2024:** $11.70  
- **2023:** $11.64  
- **2022:** $11.76

steps.args: {'prompt': 'From Netflix 2021 Form 10-K {{nflx_2021_10k_htm}}, extract consolidated/global annual revenue and global average monthly revenue per paying membership (or calculate from streaming revenue / average paying memberships / 12 if disclosed) for 2019, 2020, 2021. Also extract paid memberships end of period and average paying memberships if present. Provide exact numbers and text context.'}
steps.result:
From Netflix’s 2021 Form 10-K, Item 7, “Management’s Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations,” the relevant table states:

> “The following represents our consolidated performance highlights:”  
> “As of/ Year Ended December 31”  
> “(in thousands, except revenue per membership and percentages)”

### Extracted global / consolidated metrics

| Year | Consolidated total revenues | Streaming revenues | Paid memberships at end of period | Average paying memberships | Global average monthly revenue per paying membership |
|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| 2021 | $29,697,844 thousand | $29,515,496 thousand | 221,844 thousand | 210,784 thousand | $11.67 |
| 2020 | $24,996,056 thousand | $24,756,675 thousand | 203,663 thousand | 189,083 thousand | $10.91 |
| 2019 | $20,156,447 thousand | $19,859,230 thousand | 167,090 thousand | 152,984 thousand | $10.82 |

### Same revenue figures expressed as dollars

| Year | Consolidated total revenues | Streaming revenues |
|---:|---:|---:|
| 2021 | $29,697,844,000 | $29,515,496,000 |
| 2020 | $24,996,056,000 | $24,756,675,000 |
| 2019 | $20,156,447,000 | $19,859,230,000 |

### Calculation check for global average monthly revenue per paying membership

Netflix disclosed the global average monthly revenue per paying membership directly, but it also reconciles to:

\[
\text{Average monthly revenue per paying membership} =
\frac{\text{Streaming revenues}}{\text{Average paying memberships} \times 12}
\]

| Year | Calculation | Result |
|---:|---|---:|
| 2021 | $29,515,496k / 210,784k / 12 | $11.67 |
| 2020 | $24,756,675k / 189,083k / 12 | $10.91 |
| 2019 | $19,859,230k / 152,984k / 12 | $10.82 |

Context note: Netflix’s “average monthly revenue per paying membership” is tied to **global streaming memberships**, while “total revenues” include both streaming revenue and DVD revenue.

steps.args:
{'final_result': 'Netflix’s Average Revenue Per Paying User (ARPPU) increased modestly from 2019 to 2024. Netflix reports the comparable metric as **“average monthly revenue per paying membership”** (ARM), which is effectively monthly ARPPU per paid membership.

### Netflix ARPPU / ARM trend, 2019–2024

| Year | Average monthly revenue per paying membership | YoY change | Annualized ARPPU / ARM |
|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| 2019 | $10.82 | — | $129.84 |
| 2020 | $10.91 | +0.83% | $130.92 |
| 2021 | $11.67 | +6.97% | $140.04 |
| 2022 | $11.76 | +0.77% | $141.12 |
| 2023 | $11.64 | -1.02% | $139.68 |
| 2024 | $11.70 | +0.52% | $140.40 |

### Overall change from 2019 to 2024

Using Netflix’s disclosed monthly ARM figures:

- **2019:** $10.82 per month
- **2024:** $11.70 per month
- **Absolute increase:** $0.88 per month
- **Percentage increase:** ($11.70 / $10.82) - 1 = **8.13%**
- **Annualized increase:** $140.40 - $129.84 = **$10.56 per paying membership per year**
- **5-year CAGR:** approximately **1.58% per year**

### Interpretation

Netflix’s ARPPU/ARM rose from **$10.82 in 2019 to $11.70 in 2024**, an **8.13% total increase** over five years. The largest jump occurred in **2021**, when ARM increased nearly **7.00%**, likely reflecting pricing and mix effects. Since 2021, ARM has been relatively flat: it peaked at **$11.76 in 2022**, dipped to **$11.64 in 2023**, and recovered slightly to **$11.70 in 2024**.

In short: **Netflix’s average revenue per paying membership has grown, but only modestly, from 2019 to 2024; most of the company’s revenue growth over this period came from paid membership growth rather than large ARPPU expansion.**

    },
    {

      "name": "Netflix, Inc. 2021 Form 10-K"
    }
  ]
}'}
steps.result:
Netflix’s Average Revenue Per Paying User (ARPPU) increased modestly from 2019 to 2024. Netflix reports the comparable metric as **“average monthly revenue per paying membership”** (ARM), which is effectively monthly ARPPU per paid membership.

### Netflix ARPPU / ARM trend, 2019–2024

| Year | Average monthly revenue per paying membership | YoY change | Annualized ARPPU / ARM |
|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| 2019 | $10.82 | — | $129.84 |
| 2020 | $10.91 | +0.83% | $130.92 |
| 2021 | $11.67 | +6.97% | $140.04 |
| 2022 | $11.76 | +0.77% | $141.12 |
| 2023 | $11.64 | -1.02% | $139.68 |
| 2024 | $11.70 | +0.52% | $140.40 |

### Overall change from 2019 to 2024

Using Netflix’s disclosed monthly ARM figures:

- **2019:** $10.82 per month
- **2024:** $11.70 per month
- **Absolute increase:** $0.88 per month
- **Percentage increase:** ($11.70 / $10.82) - 1 = **8.13%**
- **Annualized increase:** $140.40 - $129.84 = **$10.56 per paying membership per year**
- **5-year CAGR:** approximately **1.58% per year**

### Interpretation

Netflix’s ARPPU/ARM rose from **$10.82 in 2019 to $11.70 in 2024**, an **8.13% total increase** over five years. The largest jump occurred in **2021**, when ARM increased nearly **7.00%**, likely reflecting pricing and mix effects. Since 2021, ARM has been relatively flat: it peaked at **$11.76 in 2022**, dipped to **$11.64 in 2023**, and recovered slightly to **$11.70 in 2024**.

In short: **Netflix’s average revenue per paying membership has grown, but only modestly, from 2019 to 2024; most of the company’s revenue growth over this period came from paid membership growth rather than large ARPPU expansion.**
